refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall maintain teleoperation link availability at or above the customer-defined target throughout the operational period.
refined_fr.rationale: Refining the original FR to explicitly state the performance metric (link availability) and the operational context ensures clarity and testability.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure link uptime statistics over the evaluation period and compare against the customer-defined availability target.
sub_frs: FR1: Monitor network link status continuously.
FR2: Detect and recover from link failures promptly.
FR3: Maintain redundant communication paths to ensure availability.

candidate_dps: DP1: Implement continuous network health monitoring subsystem.
DP2: Design automatic failover and recovery mechanisms for link failures.
DP3: Deploy redundant communication channels with load balancing.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Implement continuous network health monitoring subsystem.

coupling_check.notes: DP1 logically addresses FR1 with minimal coupling to other DPs at this level.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network infrastructure supports health monitoring.
Risk of delayed failure detection impacting availability.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating DP2 and DP3 in subsequent decomposition levels to fully address link availability.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Software and hardware components responsible for teleoperation link management and monitoring.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Interface for monitoring and managing the teleoperation communication link.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Interface to send link status and health data to NGHE Solution and Vehicle modules.

module_containment_scores.notes: Contains overall system logic but less focused on direct link monitoring.
Physical vehicle components involved but limited direct link monitoring responsibility.
Primary responsibility for teleoperation link monitoring and management.
Safety-related alerts may use link status but not responsible for monitoring.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Assigning DP1 to Teleoperation module centralizes link monitoring, minimizing diffusion and improving maintainability.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on Network Link interface reliability.
Risk of insufficient integration with Safety Services for critical alerts.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Ensure robust interface definitions and integration testing with Safety Services and NGHE Solution modules.

fr_dp_gate.reason: Selected DP1 logically satisfies the refined FR1 with clear verification approach and manageable assumptions.

diffusion_risks: Minimal diffusion risk as DP1 is well-contained within Teleoperation module.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores strongly support Teleoperation as the ownership candidate.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification via telemetry data and link status monitoring is feasible within Teleoperation module.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of responsibility for link availability monitoring to Teleoperation module.

recommended_revisions: Expand decomposition to include recovery and redundancy DPs (DP2, DP3) in next level.
Define detailed interface contracts between Teleoperation and Safety Services.

decision.justification: While DP1 and its IM assignment are sound, the FR refinement and DP selection should explicitly include recovery and redundancy aspects to fully meet the high safety criticality and availability requirements.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Monitor network link status continuously.
FR2: Detect and recover from link failures promptly.
FR3: Maintain redundant communication paths to ensure availability.

revise.why: To incorporate comprehensive coverage of link availability including failure detection, recovery, and redundancy.

assumptions: Network infrastructure supports health monitoring and failover.
Interfaces between modules are reliable and well-defined.
open_risks: Integration risks between Teleoperation and Safety Services.
Potential delays in failure detection impacting availability.
evidence_needed: Performance data from network health monitoring subsystem.
Test results of failover and redundancy mechanisms.
